original_abstract: As anthropogenic climate change poses an escalating threat to global ecological stability, there is an urgent need to scrutinize and mitigate the environmental footprint of the healthcare sector. Medical imaging, in particular, represents a substantial source of clinical carbon emissions. This article examines the critical environmental challenges facing the field and illustrates practical, sustainable interventions. By providing actionable examples of green practices, we aim to empower radiologists to lead decarbonization efforts and implement meaningful systemic changes within their departments.
humanized_abstract: With anthropogenic climate change on the increase and posing significant threats to the environment, it has become imperative to investigate and mitigate the health impacts associated with climate change and also assess the environmental impact of the health sector. Medical imaging is known to generate huge amounts of clinical carbon emissions. This article seeks to highlight the environmental challenges in the imaging sector, demonstrate some environmentally friendly alternatives and show ways in which radiologists can be at the forefront of reducing health care's carbon footprint and implement environmentally friendly practices within their departments.
